Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
Stopped by Brake Masters in Roseville and met with Will, the shop manager. Will is a strong Easypay advocate and demonstrated this firsthand by actively pitching Easypay to a customer on a ~$750 ticket while I was in the store, before I even engaged him. He expressed appreciation for the spiff being turned back on and referenced our intro call from a few weeks ago. Will shared that roughly 10% of their business last year, about $120,000 in volume, was done through Easypay. He stated clearly that he does not offer any other financing options and specifically mentioned he dislikes Snap due to difficulty of use. He noted that only a couple of Snap-approved customers have ever come in preapproved, but Easypay is his preferred and primary solution. Left my card, reinforced that I’m his local contact, provided pens and notepads, and printed a store QR code to place on the counter to make applications even easier.

Salesforce Note – Long Beach AutoTech
